note: Engine line coverage ratchet. Gated buckets may not drop more than maxDropPct below `measured`. Measured over the HONEST combined surface: the unit lcov UNIONed with the Phase C in-process harness lcov (scripts/merge-coverage.ts). See docs/HARDENING-9-PLAN.md H-02.
targetNote:
target.lib=90 is the autonomous coverage campaign goal for the lib/ business-logic core. The gate measures the HONEST combined number: unit lcov UNIONed with the in-process harness lcov (scripts/merge-coverage.ts). Harness route suites (src/tests/harness/) plus focused unit tests climb toward 90%. External-service wiring (better-auth, S3, pg-boss, ioredis, Bun.SQL.subscribe, OTel SDK) + defensive catch-branches remain for integration/soak suites.

2026-07-15: measured.lib jumped 88.8 -> 96.7 WITHOUT new tests. This is not inflation. merge-coverage.ts stopped counting non-executable lines: bun's lcov emits DA records for // comments and blank lines and scores them hits=0, which across src/ charged 1663 comment + 1476 blank lines (92 of 157 files) to the denominator. Hit counts were unaffected; only the denominator dropped. The number now means what it says -- percent of EXECUTABLE lib lines covered. target.lib stays 90: it is met with real headroom, and the 0.5pt ratchet is what guards erosion from here.
source: union of `bun test src/tests/unit --coverage` and `bun test src/tests/harness --coverage` lcovs via scripts/merge-coverage.ts (per-line DA hit union, non-executable lines excluded)
routesNote: `routes` is gated on the RATCHET with deliberately NO `target` entry. Rationale: every authz bug in this project's history landed in routes/ -- the 9 fixed in the security audit, the tenant-isolation campaign, the storage IDOR -- so the ratchet stops erosion where bugs actually happen. 2026-07-20: measured routes raised 59.7 -> 62.7 by adding in-process harness suites for insights (panels/shares/subscriptions), zones (pages/views/render), and approvals (submit/decide/cancel) -- 44 tests, which also surfaced+fixed two real 500s (insights share ON CONFLICT without a target; zones duplicate-slug now 409 not 500). Remaining routes gap is dominated by external-service wiring (backup=pg_dump/gzip/fs, realtime/ws/electric pub-sub, storage/media = S3) that belongs in integration/soak, not the harness -- so a hard `target` of 90 on routes is NOT reachable here and would block the 3.0.0 cut. Setting any real routes bar stays an OWNER call; until then the ratchet pins the floor. 2026-07-20 (second pass): raised further 62.7 -> 64.8 with harness suites for translations, tenants, users, permissions, import, revisions/comments -- 49 more tests, which surfaced+fixed a third duplicate-slug 500 (tenants POST now 409). Cumulative campaign 59.7 -> 64.8 (+5.1pt, 93 tests, 3 bugs). 2026-07-23 (third pass): 64.8 -> 67.1 by covering the *harness-reachable* slice of the external-service files — the real pg_dump/gzip backup create/status/download/delete + PITR path, media/storage folder+file DB-metadata CRUD and upload guards, and the realtime SSE auth gate. The residual is the genuinely un-harnessable I/O (S3 object bytes, SSE/ws stream fan-out, Electric) for integration/soak. 2026-07-23 (S3 lane): a MinIO endpoint wired into the Handler Coverage CI job now exercises the real aws4fetch upload path (storage+media), raising routes 67.0 -> 69.4. That surfaced+fixed a 5th bug: media upload used a 21-char nanoid for a UUID id column (every upload 500d). SSE/ws stream loops + Electric remain soak-only.
